interpretations.title: Results and Interpretations
rows: Shifting or displacement of normal brain structures from their usual position.
This suggests a space-occupying lesion like a large tumor or a significant bleed is pushing on the brain.

faqs.question: Is CT BRAIN PLAIN painful?
faqs.answer: No. You simply lie still for a few minutes.

faqs.question: How much radiation will I receive?
faqs.answer: About the same as 6 months of natural background radiation—very low risk.

faqs.question: Can I eat before the scan?
faqs.answer: Yes. No fasting is needed for a plain scan.

faqs.question: How soon will I get results?
What is the approximate CT brain plain cost?
faqs.answer: The cost of a **CT Brain Plain** can vary. For the most accurate and up-to-date pricing information, we recommend contacting Cadabams Diagnostics directly or visiting our official pricing page.

seo.title: CT BRAIN PLAIN at Cadabams Diagnostics | Fast & Safe Imaging
seo.description: Need a CT BRAIN PLAIN scan? Cadabams Diagnostics offers quick, low-dose imaging to detect brain injuries, strokes, and more. Book your appointment today.

test.markdown:
## CT Brain Plain Overview

## Brain CT Scan at Cadabams Diagnostics | Safe & Quick Imaging
## If your doctor has asked for a \*\*CT BRAIN PLAIN\*\*, you probably want clear answers—fast. At Cadabams Diagnostics, we use advanced 128-slice CT scanners and low-dose protocols to give your physician detailed images without long waits or extra radiation. This guide walks you through everything you can expect, from preparation to results.
## Overview
A \*\*CT BRAIN PLAIN\*\* scan creates cross-sectional pictures of the brain using X-rays and computer processing. It is the go-to test when doctors need to rule out or confirm:
- Head injuries and bleeding
- Stroke (ischaemic or haemorrhagic)
- Tumours or infections
- Hydrocephalus or skull fractures The entire procedure usually takes \*\*5–10 minutes\*\*, and you can go home right after.
## What is Brain CT Scan?
A \*\*Brain CT scan\*\* (also called \*\*CT Head Plain\*\*) is a non-invasive imaging test that:
- Uses a rotating X-ray tube and detectors to capture multiple angles.
- Produces thin “slices” that reveal bone, soft tissue, and blood vessels.
- Creates detailed 3-D reconstructions on a computer screen. Because no contrast dye is injected, \*\*CT BRAIN PLAIN\*\* is safe for patients with kidney issues or iodine allergies.
## When and Who Needs to Take a Brain CT Scan?
Your physician may order a \*\*Brain CT scan\*\* if you have:
- Sudden, severe headache
- Head trauma from a fall or accident
- New seizures or confusion
- Weakness, numbness, or vision loss
- Persistent nausea or vomiting \*\*Who can take it:\*\* Adults, children, and elderly patients—there is no age limit.

## Risks & Limitations of the CT Brain Plain

| Risk | Explanation | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| Radiation exposure | Low dose (~2 mSv) | Cadabams uses dose-reduction software |
| Pregnancy | Radiation can harm fetus | Inform staff if pregnant or breastfeeding |
| Claustrophobia | Short scan in open ring | Speak to our counsellor for relaxation tips |
| Type | Details |
|---|---|
| \*\*CT BRAIN PLAIN\*\* | No contrast; first-line for trauma or bleed |
| \*\*CT Brain with Contrast\*\* | Dye highlights tumours or infections |
| \*\*CT Angiography\*\* | Focuses on blood vessels (aneurysms) |
Cadabams Diagnostics offers all three; your doctor will choose the most appropriate.

## List of Parameters Considered During the CT Brain Plain

- Slice thickness (0.6–1 mm)
- Reconstruction algorithms (bone vs. soft tissue)
- Windowing (brain, bone, or subdural views)
- Radiation dose index (CTDI)
- Patient positioning and head fixation

## Reasons of Performing CT Brain Plain

- \*\*Stroke symptoms:\*\* To differentiate clot vs. bleed
- \*\*Head injury:\*\* Look for skull fracture or haematoma
- \*\*Chronic headache:\*\* Rule out mass lesion
- \*\*Infection:\*\* Assess abscess or meningitis
- \*\*Follow-up:\*\* Post-surgery or chemotherapy monitoring

## Benefits of the Test

- \*\*Fast diagnosis:\*\* Results ready within 30–60 minutes
- \*\*High accuracy:\*\* Detects bleeds as small as 3 mm
- \*\*Non-invasive:\*\* No needles (for plain scan)
- \*\*Accessible:\*\* Available 24×7 at Cadabams Diagnostics
- \*\*Cost-effective:\*\* Lower price than MRI for emergency cases

## Illnesses Diagnosed with CT Brain Plain

- Acute stroke
- Subdural or epidural haematoma
- Brain tumours (glioma, meningioma)
- Hydrocephalus
- Cerebral abscess
- Skull fractures
- Enlarged ventricles (normal-pressure hydrocephalus)

## Preparation for CT Brain Plain

- \*\*Fasting:\*\* Not required for CT BRAIN PLAIN
- \*\*Clothing:\*\* Remove metal hairpins, earrings, glasses
- \*\*Medications:\*\* Continue normal medicines
- \*\*Bring:\*\* Doctor’s prescription, ID proof, and previous scans (if any)
Tip: Arrive 15 minutes early for registration and safety checks.
---
## Pre-requisites
- Signed consent form
- Pregnancy test for women of child-bearing age (if unsure)
- Kidney function test only if contrast is planned (not for CT BRAIN PLAIN)
---
## Best Time to Take the Brain CT Scan
- \*\*Emergency:\*\* Immediately after head trauma or stroke symptoms
- \*\*Routine:\*\* Any time of day that suits you—no fasting or sedation needed
---
## Eligibility
| Group | Eligibility |
|---|---|
| Adults | Yes |
| Children | Yes; low-dose paediatric protocols |
| Pregnant women | Only if benefits outweigh risks; shielding provided |
| People with pacemakers | Safe; no MRI restrictions |
---
## Procedure for Taking a Brain CT Scan
1. \*\*Check-in:\*\* Verify ID and prescription at reception.
2. \*\*Safety screening:\*\* Remove metallic objects.
3. \*\*Positioning:\*\* Lie on the scanner table; head in a cushioned holder.
4. \*\*Scan:\*\* Table moves through the ring; you’ll hear soft whirring.
5. \*\*Completion:\*\* Technician reviews images for quality; you’re free to leave.
Total time on table: 3–5 minutes.
---
## Caution Before Taking the Test
- \*\*Metal implants:\*\* Inform us about dental clips or cochlear implants
- \*\*Claustrophobia:\*\* Let staff know; we can provide headphones or mild sedation
- \*\*Pregnancy:\*\* Always declare—alternative ultrasound or MRI may be considered
